meta.title: GEONYX | Water, Wastewater & Marine Infrastructure – Hydro and Anti-Corrosion Protection
meta.description: GEONYX develops mineral protective systems for drinking water, wastewater treatment plants (WWTP), sewerage, reservoirs, dams and marine infrastructure – resistant to hydrostatic pressure, chlorides, cavitation and biogenic corrosion. Long-lasting protection of concrete in aggressive water environments.

tender.text: Hydraulic structures and surfaces in contact with water (drinking, waste or marine) shall be protected with mineral geopolymer systems of the GEONYX type or equivalent. The system must provide watertightness (W20), resistance to positive and negative pressure, sulphate resistance and (where applicable) certification for contact with drinking water or chloride resistance for marine environments.

hero.description: Protection of concrete from erosion, carbonation and chloride attack. Solutions for dams, treatment plants and harbours, applicable even underwater.

scope.title: SCOPE
scope.z2: Pumping stations and sewage pumping stations
scope.z3: Drinking water reservoirs and clean water chambers
scope.z4: Dam walls, spillways and bottom outlets
scope.z5: Pressure pipelines, tunnels and pressure shafts
scope.z6: Channels, collectors and inspection manholes for wastewater
scope.z8: Harbours, quays and coastal protection structures
scope.z9: Dry docks, ship docks and dock chambers
scope.z10: Marine and river locks, ship canals and hydraulic galleries

manifesto.body: Pressure, cavitation, chlorides, sulphates and freezing attack concrete infrastructure around the clock. GEONYX offers hydraulic geopolymers that create a watertight barrier (W20), stop rebar corrosion and withstand aggressive waters. From drinking water reservoirs to treatment plants and marine quays.

b1.title: BIOGENIC CORROSION AND SULPHATES
b1.problem: In wastewater treatment plants (WWTP) hydrogen sulphide is converted to sulphuric acid by bacteria (biogenic corrosion). It corrodes the concrete, exposes the rebar and leads to structural failures.
b1.solution: GEONYX HYDRO + CHEM create a protective layer with high sulphate resistance and immunity to acids (pH 1–14). The smooth surface improves hydraulics and prevents sludge build-up.

b2.title: HYDROSTATIC PRESSURE AND FREEZING
b2.problem: Dam walls and spillways are subject to enormous water pressure, freeze/thaw cycles and water abrasion. Cracks and leaks can compromise wall stability.
b2.solution: GEONYX HYDRO provides watertightness under high pressure (W20) and freeze resistance (XF4). The system can be applied and cured underwater, allowing repairs without draining the reservoir.

b3.title: SALT, WAVES AND CHLORIDE CORROSION
b3.problem: In harbours and quays seawater drives chlorides into the concrete, reaching the rebar and causing corrosion (spalling). Waves and tides create mechanical wear.
b3.solution: GEONYX HYDRO creates a dense barrier stopping chloride migration to the rebar. The system is resistant to salt water and mechanical impact from vessels and debris.

b4.title: DRINKING WATER TANKS AND SAFETY
b4.problem: Drinking water reservoirs must be perfectly smooth to prevent bacterial retention and completely inert. Old concrete becomes rough and absorbs moisture.
b4.solution: GEONYX HYDRO is certified for contact with drinking water. The mineral matrix contains no solvents or toxins. The surface is smooth, easy to clean and prevents micro-organism growth.

b5.title: SEWERAGE AND COLLECTORS
b5.problem: Channels and collectors are exposed to aggressive wastewater, sand and biogenic sulphuric acid, which corrodes concrete in the upper part of the pipe.
b5.solution: GEONYX CHEM provides protection against acid attack, while HYDRO ensures watertightness of joints and inspection manholes. The system is resistant to abrasion from suspended solids.

b6.title: AQUAPARKS AND SWIMMING POOLS
b6.problem: Chlorine, ozone and constant moisture attack tile joints. The waterproofing beneath them often fails, leading to leaks and costly repairs.
b6.solution: GEONYX HYDRO is a jointless, monolithic system that serves simultaneously as waterproofing and a finish coating with aesthetic appearance and slip resistance. Resistant to UV and pool chemicals.

b7.title: INDUSTRIAL COOLING TOWERS
b7.problem: Process water with chemicals, high temperature and constant wetting destroy the concrete of cooling towers and process water tanks.
b7.solution: GEONYX THERM + CHEM provide resistance to temperature differentials and chemical additives in the water. The system protects rebar from corrosion and extends the service life of the structure.

b8.title: COASTAL REINFORCEMENT AND BREAKWATERS
b8.problem: Erosion from waves, salt and mechanical impact from floating objects destroy coastal protection structures. Standard concrete wears quickly in the tidal zone.
b8.solution: High-strength GEONYX ARMOR (Marine) mortars for geometry restoration and protection against wave abrasion. Resistant to sulphates and chlorides.

roi.title: INVESTMENT AUDIT
roi.r1geonyx: Class W20, 0 mm penetration at 5 Bar
roi.r2polymer: High risk of debonding and blistering
roi.r3geonyx: Possible, with divers and local repairs

applications.title: WHERE IT IS USED
applications.subtitle: From industrial treatment plants to marine lighthouses and drinking water reservoirs.
applications.a1desc: Aeration basins, settlers, channels and pumping stations.
applications.a2desc: Water bodies, towers, reservoirs and distribution manholes.
applications.a3desc: Spillways, pressure pipelines, locks and water towers.
applications.a4desc: Quay walls, piles, breakwaters and docks.
applications.a5desc: Main collectors, inspection manholes and drainage channels.
applications.a6desc: Swimming pools, water slides and surrounding areas.
applications.a7desc: Coastal protection structures, lighthouses and offshore platforms.
applications.a8desc: Cooling towers, process water tanks and neutralisation units.

workflow.title: EXECUTION PROCESS
workflow.w1desc: Inspection of the structure, measurement of carbonation, chlorides and concrete strength. Underwater inspection if required.
workflow.w2desc: Selection of GEONYX HYDRO system based on water aggression, pressure and mechanical loads.
workflow.w3desc: Hydro-blasting (2500 bar) to remove compromised concrete and clean the rebar.
workflow.w4desc: Reprofiling of spalled sections with high-strength geopolymer mortars (R4).
workflow.w5desc: Placement of GEONYX HYDRO membrane – by hand or machine (shotcrete), including underwater.
workflow.w6desc: Adhesion and watertightness tests. Issuance of certificates and warranty.

offer.title: OFFER PACKAGE
offer.description: For water utilities, municipalities and industrial operators GEONYX prepares a complete set of documents for tenders and direct assignment.
offer.qualityDesc: All documents comply with EN 1504 and national standards.
offer.i1: Technical specification for waterproofing and concrete protection
offer.i2: Details for joint treatment, penetrations and crack repair

systems.title: GEONYX SYSTEM SELECTION
systems.subtitle: For hydraulic structures GEONYX combines HYDRO, CHEM, ARMOR and REPAIR according to water type, pressure and environmental aggression.
systems.s1desc: Specialised systems for watertightness (W20), resistant to positive and negative hydrostatic pressure.
systems.s2desc: Mineral protection against biogenic corrosion, sulphates and acids in treatment plants and sewerage.
systems.s3desc: High-strength solutions for zones with cavitation, abrasion and mechanical impact in spillways and harbours.
systems.s4desc: Geopolymer mortars for structural restoration of spalled concrete and corroded rebar.
systems.s5desc: Certified systems for contact with drinking water, preventing micro-organism growth.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: CAN GEONYX SYSTEMS BE APPLIED UNDERWATER?
faq.a1: Yes, certain configurations of GEONYX HYDRO are designed for application and curing underwater by diving teams, enabling repairs to dam walls and quays without draining the reservoir.
faq.q2: ARE THE MATERIALS SAFE FOR CONTACT WITH DRINKING WATER?
faq.a2: Yes, GEONYX HYDRO holds certificates for contact with drinking water. Its mineral nature releases no toxins, contains no solvents and does not alter the taste or composition of the water.
faq.q3: WHAT IS THE RESISTANCE TO SEAWATER AND CHLORIDES?
faq.a3: Extremely high. The systems are designed to stop chloride diffusion, protecting the rebar from corrosion even in aggressive saline environments and tidal zones.

cta.title: COMPREHENSIVE SITE ZONING
cta.description: Every hydraulic structure has specific risks. We integrate specialised matrices into one system: from watertightness under pressure and chemical resistance, to abrasion resistance and drinking water contact. One technology that guarantees 100% coverage of needs.
